steps.title: Set up the equation
steps.content:
We need to find the altitude $x$ when the temperature is 16°C. Substitute $T(x) = 16$ into the given function:
$$16 = \frac{22,000 - 6.5x}{1,000}$$

steps.title: Backsolve using the multiple choice answers
steps.content:
Since the answer choices are simple integers, we can test them directly rather than solving algebraically.

Backsolving — testing choice C (1,375):
$$T(1375) = \frac{22,000 - 6.5(1375)}{1,000} = \frac{22,000 - 8,937.5}{1,000} = \frac{13,062.5}{1,000} \approx 13.06°C$$
This is less than our target of 16°C, so this doesn't work. Fails. Testing choice B (923):
$$T(923) = \frac{22,000 - 6.5(923)}{1,000} = \frac{22,000 - 5,999.5}{1,000} = \frac{16,000.5}{1,000} \approx 16.0°C$$
This matches our target temperature. That matches choice B — B is correct.
